steps.title: Express y from both equations
steps.content:
From the first equation $y + k = x + 26$:
$$y = x + 26 - k$$

From the second equation $y - k = x^2 - 5x$:
$$y = x^2 - 5x + k$$

Any solution to the system must satisfy both equations, so these expressions for $y$ must be equal.

steps.title: Combine into a single equation in x
steps.content:
Set the two expressions for $y$ equal:
$$x + 26 - k = x^2 - 5x + k$$

Rearrange to standard form:
$$0 = x^2 - 5x - x + k + k - 26$$
$$x^2 - 6x + (2k - 26) = 0$$

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition
steps.content:
For the system to have exactly one solution, this quadratic in $x$ must have exactly one real root. This occurs when the discriminant equals zero:
$$b^2 - 4ac = 0$$
$$(-6)^2 - 4(1)(2k - 26) = 0$$
$$36 - 8k + 104 = 0$$
$$140 = 8k$$
$$k = 17.5$$
